title: Molten Comforting Uruguayan Sour Cheese Empanadas
description: Flaky pastry pockets filled with a tangy, creamy cheese mixture that's perfect for a cozy evening in.

ingredients.name: Vegan Egg Replacer
ingredients.notes: To replace eggs in the dough.

instructions: Roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
In a small bowl, mix together the cheese, mustard, tapioca flour, kombu powder, salt, black pepper, and garlic powder.
Brush the edges of the dough circles with water, then spoon a small amount of the cheese mixture onto one half of each circle.
Fold the other half of the dough over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada.
Use a fork to crimp the edges and create a decorative border.
Place the empanadas on the preheated grill, leaving about 1 inch of space between each one.
Cook the empanadas for 10-12 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is melted and bubbly.
Remove the empanadas from the grill and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
